scenario: Buying a monthly travel pass at the metro ticket kiosk

transcript.text: Здравствуйте. Мне нужен проездной на месяц.
transcript.romaji: Zdrávstvuyte. Mnye núzhen prayezdnóy na myésyats.

transcript.text: Хорошо. Это будет две тысячи рублей.
transcript.romaji: Kharashó. Éta búdyet dvye týsyachi rublyéy.

transcript.text: А можно жетон, если у меня закончится проездной?
transcript.romaji: A mózhna zhetón, yésli u menyá zakónchitsya prayezdnóy?

transcript.text: И пополните, пожалуйста, мою Тройку на пятьсот рублей.
transcript.romaji: I papólnite, pazhálusta, mayú Tróyku na pyat'sót rublyéy.

translation.text: Hello! What do you need?
Hello. I need a monthly travel pass.
Okay. That'll be two thousand rubles.
And can I get a token in case my pass runs out?
Yes, a token costs fifty rubles.
And please top up my Troika card by five hundred rubles.
Okay, tap your card on the terminal.

grammarNotes: Мне нужен uses the dative-of-need construction (мне = 'to me', нужен agrees in gender with the noun needed — нужен for masculine проездной, нужна for feminine, нужно for neuter) rather than a verb 'to need'.

scenario: Asking a passerby which line to transfer to

transcript.text: Извините, подскажите, пожалуйста, где пересадка на кольцевую линию?
transcript.romaji: Izviníte, patskazhýte, pazhálusta, gdye peresátka na kal'tsevúyu líniyu?

translation.text: Excuse me, could you tell me, please, where's the transfer to the circle line?
The transfer is at the next station, to the right.
And what's the next station?
The next station is — Kurskaya.
Thank you very much!
You're welcome!

grammarNotes: На следующей станции uses the prepositional case (станции) after на to show location — compare the nominative станция used when just naming the station in isolation.

scenario: Asking a fellow passenger which station is next

transcript.text: Точно не знаю, посмотрите на схему метро.
transcript.romaji: Tóchna nye znáyu, pasmatríte na skhému myetró.

translation.text: Excuse me, what's the next station?
The next station is — Mayakovskaya.
And after that one?
I don't know for sure, take a look at the metro map.
Okay, thanks!

grammarNotes: После неё ('after it') uses the genitive pronoun неё referring back to станция (feminine) — Russian pronouns must match the grammatical gender of the noun they replace, not any real-world gender.

translation.text: For the fare, please pass it along.
Sure, passing it along.
Here's your change from the driver.
Thank you very much!

grammarNotes: No one hands money to the driver directly in a crowded маршрутка — передайте, пожалуйста ('please pass this along') asks whoever's in front of you to relay it seat by seat, and change comes back the same trust-based way.

scenario: Asking the marshrutka driver to stop at a specific spot

transcript.text: Эта остановка по требованию, скажите заранее в следующий раз.
transcript.romaji: Éta astanófka pa trébavaniyu, skazhýte zaranyéye v slyéduyushchiy raz.

translation.text: Please stop by the pharmacy!
This is a stop-on-request, let me know ahead of time next time.
Okay, sorry. This is my stop.
No problem, I'll stop now.

grammarNotes: Marshrutka stops are frequently «по требованию» (on request) rather than fixed — announce where you want off a little in advance, since the driver may already be past the spot by the time you call out.

scenario: Asking for directions to the nearest metro entrance

transcript.text: Извините, подскажите, пожалуйста, где ближайший вход в метро?
transcript.romaji: Izviníte, patskazhýte, pazhálusta, gdye blizháyshiy fkhot v myetró?

translation.text: Excuse me, could you tell me, please, where's the nearest metro entrance?
Go straight, then turn right.
And is that far from here?
No, five minutes on foot.
Thank you very much!

grammarNotes: Идите (go) is the imperative form of идти, addressed with the vы-implied polite form — requests to strangers default to this form rather than the informal singular иди.

scenario: A confused tourist getting help on the platform

transcript.text: Извините, я заблудился. Как пройти к выходу?
transcript.romaji: Izviníte, ya zabludílsya. Kak praytí k výkhadu?

transcript.text: А мне нужно на «Театральную». Это правильное направление?
transcript.romaji: A mnye núzhna na Teatrál'nuyu. Éta právil'naye napravlyéniye?

transcript.text: Нет, вам нужна другая платформа, через переход.
transcript.romaji: Nyet, vam nuzhná drugáya platfórma, chyerez perekhót.

translation.text: Excuse me, I'm lost. How do I get to the exit?
The exit is over there, past the escalator.
And I need to get to Teatralnaya. Is this the right direction?
No, you need a different platform, through the passageway.
Thank you, you've helped me a lot!

grammarNotes: Заблудился marks the speaker as male (заблудилась would be used by a woman) — Russian past-tense verbs always agree with the subject's gender in the singular, unlike English 'I got lost,' which carries no such marking.
